name: Gelada
primary_sources: International Union for Conservation of Nature

visual_attributes.morphology: large and robust primate with a short muzzle and hairless face
distinctive_features: bright red hourglass-shaped patch of skin on the chest
long, heavy cape of hair on the back of adult males
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: males average 18.5 kg (41 lb), females average 11 kg (24 lb), head-body length 50–75 cm (20–30 in), tail 30–50 cm (12–20 in)
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: short tail ending in a tuft of hair, arms and feet nearly black

domain_specific_visual.SEXUAL_DIMORPHISM: males larger with more pronounced hair cape and chest patch
domain_specific_visual.CHEST_PATCH: hourglass-shaped, bright red in males, less pronounced in females except during estrus

domain_specific_functional.SOCIAL_STRUCTURE: complex multilevel social structure with reproductive and male units

relationships.description: Geladas are found only in the high grasslands of the Ethiopian plateau.
Two subspecies of gelada exist: Northern gelada and Eastern gelada.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: current species, with fossil records dating back to prehistoric times
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: unique primate species with complex social behaviors and vocalizations
primary_sources: International Union for Conservation of Nature

contrastive_constraints: NOT lacking the bright red chest patch
NOT found outside the Ethiopian Highlands
